1.11a: Contact the specific Mortgage Operations office that issued the instructions.
1.11b: Contact the specific Mortgage Operations office that issued the instructions.
1.7: Lending is prohibited if the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: Legal firms may act if the seller is the transaction handler or family, provided a separate partner or fee earner acts under supervision.
1.15: Legal firms may act if the borrower is the transaction handler or family, provided a separate fee earner acts under supervision, professional guidelines are met, and no conflict exists.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's legal representative details is only required if specifically requested.
3.1.4: Seller must have legal representation if an existing first charge is registered, unless the seller is a Housing Association or Local Authority.
In all other cases, refer to the instructing Mortgage Operations Office.
3.1.5: No alternative documents are accepted for identity verification.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's legal representative details is only required if specifically requested.
3.2.4: Seller must have legal representation if an existing first charge is registered, unless the seller is a Housing Association or Local Authority.
In all other cases, refer to the instructing Mortgage Operations Office.
4.5a: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
4.5b: Certificates of title must be submitted via the Lender Exchange portal.
4.1: A valuation report is usually provided by the lender as part of the instructions.
4.3: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office regarding incorrect valuation assumptions.
5.1.1: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.2.1: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office if the seller is not the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Do not send environmental or contaminated land reports unless specifically requested in the valuation report.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm subscribes to the Search Code monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board.
5.4.6: Search insurance is not accepted for Land Registry searches.
Search insurance is accepted for other required searches at the conveyancer's risk.
5.5.3a: Report breaches of planning conditions or unsatisfied completion conditions to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.5.3b: Planning permission copies are not required unless explicitly specified in individual instructions.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consent copies are not required unless explicitly specified in individual instructions.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required unless explicitly specified in individual instructions.
5.5.4: Report property restrictions affecting value or marketability to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted subject to Section 5.7.2 requirements.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted unless they meet specific Handbook criteria (5.8.1 to 5.8.5).
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds or freehold flats to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.8.1: Security for buildings converted into maximum four flats is accepted if the borrower occupies one unit and the borrower (or another flat owner) owns the freehold.
5.8.5: Accepted if the borrower owns the freehold reversion of the other flat in a two-flat building and vice versa, provided letting is agreed from completion.
Not accepted if the borrower owns both flats.
5.10.1: Use restrictions are acceptable only if the mortgage offer or instructions confirm lender awareness.
Otherwise, report to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.13.1: Refer to the instructing Mortgage Operations office if the borrower is not providing the full balance from own funds or is proposing a second charge.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee or insolvent landlords requires prior approval from the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.14.17a: Building safety documentation does not need to be sent.
5.14.17b: Notify the lender if remediation cost assumptions in the valuation report are incorrect.
5.14.17c: Notify the lender if remediation cost assumptions in the valuation report are incorrect for remortgages.
5.14.1: Report lease terms if: valuer assumed 55-82 years but actual term differs by >1 year; valuer assumed >82 years but actual is <82 years; or no valuation was provided.
Minimum remaining term at mortgage expiry: 50 years for interest-only, 30 years for capital and interest.
Leasehold Reform Act 1993 extensions are accepted if statutory compensation is available.
5.14.9: Ground rent is acceptable if: <= 0.1% of property value, review period >= 10 years, and increases <= RPI.
If ground rent > 0.1% of value, refer to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
Review periods < 10 years or increases > RPI require a deed of variation.
5.14.10: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office regarding service charge matters.
5.14.11: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office regarding indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts do not need to be sent.
5.14.13: Receipted notice of assignment or evidence of service does not need to be sent.
5.15.2a: Report management company issues to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office if unable to certify that search entries do not relate to the property.
5.17.5a: Send a certified copy of the Power of Attorney and a covering letter within 7 days of completion.
Verify delegated powers and confirm the attorney's address and relationship to donor.
5.17.5b: Send certified copies of the Statutory Declaration of Non-Revocation and Power of Attorney within 7 days of completion.
Verify delegated powers and confirm the attorney's address and relationship to donor.
5.19.1: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership requirements.
5.20.1: Report solar PV roof space leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
5.20.3: Notify the instructing Mortgage Operations office of any solar PV roof space lease and provide the tenant's name.
5.20.4: Disclose Green Deal Plans only if there are unusual situations or concerns.
6.1.3: Notify the instructing Mortgage Operations office if the borrower is not proceeding with the offer.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.4.4: Incentives do not need reporting if: seller is a developer and cash incentive is <= 5% of price, or covers legal fees/SDLT.
Standard specs (carpets, white goods), chancel repair, and defective title insurance premiums do not need reporting.
All other incentives must be reported.
Report Property Investment Club involvement or finder's fees and await instructions.
6.4.5: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office if the conveyancer will not control the payment of all purchase money.
6.5.1: Report lack of vacant possession to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.6.1: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office regarding let properties and buy-to-let eligibility.
6.6.2: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office if current or completion letting details are missing.
6.6.3: Certified copy tenancy agreements are not required unless specified in instructions.
6.6.4: Lending is prohibited on properties subject to mandatory HMO licensing.
6.7.1: Acceptable schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, BLP, BOPAS, LABC, Build Zone, Build Assure, Global Home Warranties, Q Assure Build, Aedis/Homeproof, ICW, Protek, Ark Residential, ABC+, One Guarantee, Advantage (ACHI), Cadis, CIR, Checkmate, Compariqo.
6.7.2: New home warranty documents should be retained by the borrower.
6.7.3: Building standards indemnity assignments are not required unless specified in instructions.
6.7.4: PCC is accepted for single properties or developments up to 10 units if supervised from the start by a qualified professional (excluding FASI/MASI).
PCC must use the exact UKF template wording.
Converted flats (max 4 units) without warranty are accepted if planning/building regs/completion certificates are present.
6.7.6: Professional Consultant’s Certificates do not need to be sent unless specified.
6.8.1: Refer to Mortgage Operations only if: developer refuses to covenant for road/sewer completion, it is a private sale, or LTV > 85% and developer is not LSE-listed or a subsidiary of an LSE-listed company.
6.9.1: Report missing easements to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.10.2: Retentions or mortgage instalments are released to the borrower.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
7.3: Signed consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or over.
7.4: Report doubts about disclosure accuracy to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity policies do not need to be sent; provide insurer name, policy number, and type only.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the purchase price or 110% of the mortgage advance (whichever is lower).
10.2a: Mortgage advance is paid via electronic transfer.
10.2b: A minimum of 5 days notice is required for the release of funds.
10.3: Standard deductions for fees are detailed in the individual mortgage offer.
10.7: Funds for delayed/cancelled completions must be returned immediately via CHAPS.
Reference must include borrower name and full mortgage account number.
10.9: Report delayed completion to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
10.10: Advance funds can be held for a maximum of 4 days.
Unreturned funds may incur interest at Santander's full reversion rate.
10.11: If holding funds with lender consent, interest is payable at the greater of 50% of the reversion rate or the actual interest received.
Unauthorized retention of funds incurs interest at the full reversion rate.
12.3.1: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office for release of retentions or instalments.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds do not need to be sent; retain the original if using e-DRS.
14.2.1: Title deeds address: Santander UK plc, Mortgage & Loans Operations, Sunderland, SR43 4FH.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents required: Land Registry Title Information Document, certified PoA/Statutory Declarations, and Deeds of Consent or Guarantee (if separate from standard deed).
16.1.1: Contact the Mortgage Operations office managing the borrower's account.
16.3.7a: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office to obtain execution of transfer of equity.
16.3.7b: The lender uses a specific common seal attestation clause for Santander UK plc.
16.3.1: No standard transfer/deed form; contact Mortgage Operations for required wording.
16.3.2: Contact the instructing Mortgage Operations office for debt amount confirmation.
16.3.4: A certified copy of the transfer of equity must be sent to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.4.1: Submit applications for consent to letting to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.4.2: Proposed tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
16.5.3a: Send deeds of variation to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.5.3b: Send deeds of rectification to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.5.3c: Send deeds of easement to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.5.3d: Send option agreements to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
16.5.2: Consult the instructing Mortgage Operations office to confirm if a deed will adversely affect security.
17.1.1: Redemption statements must be requested from the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
17.2.1a: Send the discharge and repayment remittance to the instructing Mortgage Operations office.
17.2.1b: Discharge method (DS1 or direct Land Registry) is specified in individual instructions.
